Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:subst:PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]]

revision.comment: 25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小物忌神社 up to rev 83984308
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|山形県酒田市'''山楯'''の小物忌神社|同市'''飛島'''の同名神社|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)}} {{神社 |名称 = 小物忌神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|小物忌神社　拝殿]]
小物忌神社　拝殿 |所在地 = 山形県酒田市山楯字三之宮48 |位置 = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|E|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=小物忌神社}} |祭神 = [[シナツヒコ|級長津比古命]]、[[シナツヒコ|級長津比賣命]]、[[トヨウケビメ|豊受比賣命]] |社格 = 式内社（小）・[[出羽国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]]・旧県社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月17日]] |神事 = 天狗舞神楽 |地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=小物忌神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|一の鳥居
[[国道345号]]の交差点付近にある一の鳥居。]] [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|本殿]] [[ファイル:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|夢草堂]] '''小物忌神社'''（おものいみじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[酒田市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[出羽国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。酒田市内には同名の神社が2カ所にある。 == 祭神 == * [[シナツヒコ|級長津比古命]] * [[シナツヒコ|級長津比賣命]] * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受比賣命]] == 由緒 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝では[[景行天皇]]時代に[[武内宿禰]]が官籍に載せたとされている。[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に刊行された『飽海郡誌　巻之六』によれば、もとは「三之宮大座明神」と称し、古来より[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]の第3王子とであるとされ、鳥海山大物忌神社が「春物忌」を行った後に当社においても3日間の「物忌」を行うのが慣わしになっていたと言う。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）の『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に小社として記載されているほか、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）に[[従五位|従五位下]][[勲等|勲七等]]の[[神階]]に昇叙され『[[日本三代実録]]』8月4日条。、翌々年の同4年（[[880年]]）に更に従五位上に昇叙された『日本三代実録』2月27日条。「袁物忌神」に相当すると見られている。 『山形縣神社誌』山形県神社庁五十周年記念事業実行委員会出版部 編　『山形縣神社誌』　山形県神社庁　2000年4月　より。では、[[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]）[[源義家]]が東征の際、賊軍の勢いが強く戦が不利になった為、家臣の首藤主馬を大物忌神社と小物忌神社に使わして戦勝を祈願させた。やがて敵陣を陥落させた後、[[太刀|大刀]]と[[鏑矢]]を奉納した、との社伝を紹介している。 16世紀前期には、この地方の領主[[武藤氏]]一門の[[砂越城|砂越（さごし）城主]]に[[社領]]を没収され、[[天正|天正年間]]には兵火により社殿が焼失し荒廃していた。『風土略記』には、兵火に罹って社殿神体が焼失し、残った台座を新殿に安置したので「大座明神」と呼ばれた、との言い伝えが記載されている。しかしながら『風土略記』では、この説の真偽は不明であり、社名の由来としては不敬な称呼ではないか、吹浦の古い縁起にも大座とあるので、兵火による焼失後の呼称では無いだろうと考察している。 [[江戸時代]]に入り[[元文]]元年（[[1736年]]）[[庄内藩]]主[[酒井氏]]により復興された。『出羽風土略記』によれば、[[社家]]も無く、三之宮村の村役人が守護してきたが、山楯村の長久寺が[[別当]]となって[[宝暦]]9年（[[1759年]]）経堂を建て、1宇の仏壇を構えて念仏修行の場としたと言う。さらに観音とのみ称して社号を捨てたため、公儀の諸国神社調査からも洩れてしまったとされる。『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』谷川健一 編　『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』　㈱白水社　1984年6月　より。においても、再建はされたが長久寺の力が強く、念仏道場として観音と呼ばれていた、と述べている。 『飽海郡誌　巻之六』によれば、[[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に新たに3神を祭り、初めて小物忌神社と公称した。同年[[2月24日]][[村社]]に列格、同年[[11月11日]][[郷社]]に列せられると同時に[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神社]]の[[摂社]]に指定された。しかしながら、明治32年（[[1899年]]）[[3月26日]]に社殿が焼失。すぐに再建に着手し、明治34年（[[1901年]]）[[11月24日]]に竣功、遷宮式を行った。明治35年（[[1902年]]）[[3月25日]][[県社]]に列せられた、としている。 === 年表 === * [[景行天皇]]時代 - [[武内宿禰]]により官籍に記録 * [[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）8月 - 従五位下勲七等に昇叙 *:[[元慶の乱|秋田夷乱（元慶の乱）]]において朝廷軍が敗退したのを受け占ったところ、古来より征戦に霊験を有する[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神]]、[[出羽三山|月山神]]、小物忌神の3神が、神気賊に帰して祈祷が届かなくなってしまったと出た。そこで3社の爵級を増せば霊応あるべしとして、小物忌神は勳九等を勳七等に進められた。 * 元慶4年（[[880年]]） - 従五位上に昇叙 * [[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]） - 源義家戦勝祈願と伝えられる * 明治9年（1876年）2月24日 - 村社 * 同年[[11月11日]] - 郷社、[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神社]][[摂社]] * 明治35年（[[1902年]]）[[3月25日]] - 県社 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[羽越本線]][[砂越駅]]から自動車で約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[佐伯有義]] 編　『神祇全書　第1輯』　[[皇典講究所]] 1906年10月　（1971年に思文閣より複製版が出されている） *[[谷川健一]] 編　『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』　[[白水社|㈱白水社]]　1984年6月 *神道大系編纂会編　『[[神道大系]]　神社編28　出羽国』　神道大系編纂会　1990年3月 *山形県神社庁五十周年記念事業実行委員会出版部 編　『山形縣神社誌』　山形県神社庁　2000年4月 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} *[[小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] - 酒田市内にある同名神社。同じく[[式内社論社]]とされる。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.omonoimi.com/ 小物忌神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おものいみしんしや}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:出羽国の式内社]] [[Category:山形県の旧県社]] [[Category:酒田市の歴史]] [[Category:出羽国|社3]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]]

{{Otheruses|山形県酒田市'''山楯'''の小物忌神社|同市'''飛島'''の同名神社|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)}} {{神社 |名称 = 小物忌神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|小物忌神社　拝殿]]
小物忌神社　拝殿 |所在地 = 山形県酒田市山楯字三之宮48 |位置 = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|E|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=小物忌神社}} |祭神 = [[シナツヒコ|級長津比古命]]、[[シナツヒコ|級長津比賣命]]、[[トヨウケビメ|豊受比賣命]] |社格 = 式内社（小）・[[出羽国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]]・旧県社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月17日]] |神事 = 天狗舞神楽 |地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=小物忌神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|一の鳥居
[[国道345号]]の交差点付近にある一の鳥居。]] [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|本殿]] [[ファイル:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|夢草堂]] '''小物忌神社'''（おものいみじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[酒田市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[出羽国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。酒田市内には同名の神社が2カ所にある。 == 祭神 == * [[シナツヒコ|級長津比古命]] * [[シナツヒコ|級長津比賣命]] * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受比賣命]] == 由緒 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝では[[景行天皇]]時代に[[武内宿禰]]が官籍に載せたとされている。[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に刊行された『飽海郡誌　巻之六』によれば、もとは「三之宮大座明神」と称し、古来より[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]の第3王子とであるとされ、鳥海山大物忌神社が「春物忌」を行った後に当社においても3日間の「物忌」を行うのが慣わしになっていたと言う。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）の『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に小社として記載されているほか、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）に[[従五位|従五位下]][[勲等|勲七等]]の[[神階]]に昇叙され『[[日本三代実録]]』8月4日条。、翌々年の同4年（[[880年]]）に更に従五位上に昇叙された『日本三代実録』2月27日条。「袁物忌神」に相当すると見られている。 『山形縣神社誌』山形県神社庁五十周年記念事業実行委員会出版部 編　『山形縣神社誌』　山形県神社庁　2000年4月　より。では、[[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]）[[源義家]]が東征の際、賊軍の勢いが強く戦が不利になった為、家臣の首藤主馬を大物忌神社と小物忌神社に使わして戦勝を祈願させた。やがて敵陣を陥落させた後、[[太刀|大刀]]と[[鏑矢]]を奉納した、との社伝を紹介している。 16世紀前期には、この地方の領主[[武藤氏]]一門の[[砂越城|砂越（さごし）城主]]に[[社領]]を没収され、[[天正|天正年間]]には兵火により社殿が焼失し荒廃していた。『風土略記』には、兵火に罹って社殿神体が焼失し、残った台座を新殿に安置したので「大座明神」と呼ばれた、との言い伝えが記載されている。しかしながら『風土略記』では、この説の真偽は不明であり、社名の由来としては不敬な称呼ではないか、吹浦の古い縁起にも大座とあるので、兵火による焼失後の呼称では無いだろうと考察している。 [[江戸時代]]に入り[[元文]]元年（[[1736年]]）[[庄内藩]]主[[酒井氏]]により復興された。『出羽風土略記』によれば、[[社家]]も無く、三之宮村の村役人が守護してきたが、山楯村の長久寺が[[別当]]となって[[宝暦]]9年（[[1759年]]）経堂を建て、1宇の仏壇を構えて念仏修行の場としたと言う。さらに観音とのみ称して社号を捨てたため、公儀の諸国神社調査からも洩れてしまったとされる。『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』谷川健一 編　『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』　㈱白水社　1984年6月　より。においても、再建はされたが長久寺の力が強く、念仏道場として観音と呼ばれていた、と述べている。 『飽海郡誌　巻之六』によれば、[[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に新たに3神を祭り、初めて小物忌神社と公称した。同年[[2月24日]][[村社]]に列格、同年[[11月11日]][[郷社]]に列せられると同時に[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神社]]の[[摂社]]に指定された。しかしながら、明治32年（[[1899年]]）[[3月26日]]に社殿が焼失。すぐに再建に着手し、明治34年（[[1901年]]）[[11月24日]]に竣功、遷宮式を行った。明治35年（[[1902年]]）[[3月25日]][[県社]]に列せられた、としている。 === 年表 === * [[景行天皇]]時代 - [[武内宿禰]]により官籍に記録 * [[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）8月 - 従五位下勲七等に昇叙 *:[[元慶の乱|秋田夷乱（元慶の乱）]]において朝廷軍が敗退したのを受け占ったところ、古来より征戦に霊験を有する[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神]]、[[出羽三山|月山神]]、小物忌神の3神が、神気賊に帰して祈祷が届かなくなってしまったと出た。そこで3社の爵級を増せば霊応あるべしとして、小物忌神は勳九等を勳七等に進められた。 * 元慶4年（[[880年]]） - 従五位上に昇叙 * [[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]） - 源義家戦勝祈願と伝えられる * 明治9年（1876年）2月24日 - 村社 * 同年[[11月11日]] - 郷社、[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神社]][[摂社]] * 明治35年（[[1902年]]）[[3月25日]] - 県社 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[羽越本線]][[砂越駅]]から自動車で約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[佐伯有義]] 編　『神祇全書　第1輯』　[[皇典講究所]] 1906年10月　（1971年に思文閣より複製版が出されている） *[[谷川健一]] 編　『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』　[[白水社|㈱白水社]]　1984年6月 *神道大系編纂会編　『[[神道大系]]　神社編28　出羽国』　神道大系編纂会　1990年3月 *山形県神社庁五十周年記念事業実行委員会出版部 編　『山形縣神社誌』　山形県神社庁　2000年4月 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} *[[小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] - 酒田市内にある同名神社。同じく[[式内社論社]]とされる。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.omonoimi.com/ 小物忌神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おものいみしんしや}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:出羽国の式内社]] [[Category:山形県の旧県社]] [[Category:酒田市の歴史]] [[Category:出羽国|社3]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:subst:PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|12=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} ]] }} {{draft categories| [[Category:subst:PAGENAME}} [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} ]] }} {{draft categories| [[Category:subst:PAGENAME}} [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} ]] }} {{draft categories| [[Category:subst:PAGENAME}} [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]]

Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} ]] }} {{draft categories| [[Category:subst:PAGENAME}} [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]]

revision.comment: 112 revisions imported: re-importing this because I accidentally imported the wrong jawiki history
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]]

revision.comment: 17 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島) up to rev 85080034
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小物忌神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|小物忌神社　拝殿]]
小物忌神社　拝殿 |所在地 = [[山形県]][[酒田市]]飛島字中村甲178 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=小物忌神社}} |祭神 = [[シナツヒコ|級長津彦命]]、[[シナツヒコ|級長戸辺命]] |社格 = 式内小社論社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[切妻造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月20日]] |神事 = 鳥海山火合せ神事（[[7月14日]]） |地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=小物忌神社|position=right|width=200|float=center}} }} '''小物忌神社'''（おものいみじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[酒田市]]の離島、[[飛島 (山形県)|飛島]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社昭和21年（[[1946年]]）1月に[[県社]]昇格に該当するものと認められた（『神社名鑑』）。『山形縣神社誌』には、大宮神社であった時代に[[村社]]あるいは県社に列せられていたような記述が見られる。。なお、酒田市内には[[小物忌神社|同名の神社]]がもう1社ある。 == 祭神 == * [[シナツヒコ|級長津彦命]] * [[シナツヒコ|級長戸辺命]] == 由緒 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝では[[大和国]][[龍田大社]]からの[[勧請]]であるという。[[式内社論社|式内社の論社]]とされ、『[[神名帳考証]]』巻5には『神名帳考証』は『神祇全書 第1輯』所収。、小物忌神社が今は飛島にあり、伝承によれば[[祭神]]は[[風神]]で、『[[古事記]]』に言う[[ワクムスビ|和久産巣日神]]であると記載されている風神は龍田大社の祭神と同じであるが、『古事記』における風の神は'''志那都比古神'''である。志那都比古神は、『[[日本書紀]]』において'''級長津彦命'''と表記されるが、[[伊奘諾尊]]が朝霧を吹き払った息から生まれた'''級長戸辺命'''（しなとべのみこと）の別名であるとされる（四神出生段一書第6）。和久産巣日神は五穀・養蚕の神で、[[トヨウケビメ|豊受比売命]]の親神である。。 『山形縣神社誌』によれば、[[元文]]3年（[[1738年]]）社名を大宮神社と改めたところ、小物忌神社の名を獲得しようと至るところに小物忌神社を称する神社が現れたのだと言う。さらに同書によれば、何の証左も無く小物忌神社を称する神社が続出したことを慨した酒田市財団法人光丘文庫長の白崎良弥が、私費を投じて調査した結果、飛島の大宮神社こそが小物忌神社であることを突き止め、陳情活動などの末、[[昭和]]17年（[[1942年]]）[[7月10日]]付けにて社名を小物忌神社へ変更することが認められたとしている。 === 年表 === * 龍田大社からの勧請という * [[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）8月 - 従五位下勲九等（但し、式内小物忌神社に対して） * 元慶4年（[[880年]]） - 従五位上昇叙（同上） * 元文3年（1738年） - 社名を大宮神社と改める * 昭和17年（1942年）7月10日 - 社名を小物忌神社に復する == 境内の風景 == ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|'''小物忌神社　鳥居''' ファイル:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|'''小物忌神社　境内''' ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|'''小物忌神社　本殿''' == 交通 == * [[酒田港]]から定期船[[とびしま]]で勝浦港に移動（1日1便、航行時間約75分）後、徒歩約20分 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[佐伯有義]] 編　『神祇全書　第1輯』　[[皇典講究所]] 1906年10月　（1971年に思文閣より複製版が出されている） *[[谷川健一]] 編　『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』　[[白水社]]　1984年6月 *神道大系編纂会編　『神道大系　神社編28　出羽国』　神道大系編纂会　1990年3月 *山形県神社庁五十周年記念事業実行委員会出版部 編　『山形縣神社誌』　山形県神社庁　2000年4月 *神社本庁調査部編『神社名鑑』、[[神社本庁]]、昭和38年 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おものいみしんしや}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:出羽国の式内社]] [[Category:山形県の旧郷社]] [[Category:酒田市の歴史]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] to [[Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

従五位下勲九等（但し、式内小物忌神社に対して） * 元慶4年（[[880年]]） - 従五位上昇叙（同上） * 元文3年（1738年） - 社名を大宮神社と改める * 昭和17年（1942年）7月10日 - 社名を小物忌神社に復する == 境内の風景 == ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|'''小物忌神社　鳥居''' ファイル:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|'''小物忌神社　境内''' ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|'''小物忌神社　本殿''' == 交通 == * [[酒田港]]から定期船[[とびしま]]で勝浦港に移動（1日1便、航行時間約75分）後、徒歩約20分 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[佐伯有義]] 編　『神祇全書　第1輯』　[[皇典講究所]] 1906年10月　（1971年に思文閣より複製版が出されている） *[[谷川健一]] 編　『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』　[[白水社]]　1984年6月 *神道大系編纂会編　『神道大系　神社編28　出羽国』　神道大系編纂会　1990年3月 *山形県神社庁五十周年記念事業実行委員会出版部 編　『山形縣神社誌』　山形県神社庁　2000年4月 *神社本庁調査部編『神社名鑑』、[[神社本庁]]、昭和38年 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おものいみしんしや}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:出羽国の式内社]] [[Category:山形県の旧郷社]] [[Category:酒田市の歴史]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Port|qq=}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata|qq=}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai-sha|qq=}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobishima (Yamagata)|qq=}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai-sha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|qq=}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|qq=}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Port|qq=}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Port|qq=}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata|qq=}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobishima (Yamagata)|qq=}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|qq=}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|qq=}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Port|qq=}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|qq=}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|qq=}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]]

In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Sakata]]
