revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[:en:Tobishima, Yamagata|Tobishima (Yamagata)]], an isolated island in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|qq=}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|qq=}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]]

It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|qq=}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|qq=}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Tobishima}}

Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Tobishima}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[:en:Tobishima, Yamagata|Tobishima (Yamagata)]], an isolated island in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|qq=}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワクムスビ}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Tobishima}}

Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[:en:Tobishima, Yamagata|Tobishima (Yamagata)]], an isolated island in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|qq=}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワクムスビ}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|version=85080034|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Tobishima}}

Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[:en:Tobishima, Yamagata|Tobishima (Yamagata)]], an isolated island in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was District Shrine.<ref>In January
1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine.</ref> Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City.
== Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588824}}, Volume 5,''<ref>''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.''</ref> which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワクムスビ}}.<ref>The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>
According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942.
=== Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine.
== Shrine Grounds == <gallery widths="200px" heights="150px" perrow="3"> File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall </gallery>
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes.
== Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963.
{{Omonoimi Faith}}

Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was District Shrine.<ref>In January
1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine.</ref> Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City.
== Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588824}}, Volume 5,''<ref>''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.''</ref> which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワクムスビ}}.<ref>The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>
According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942.
=== Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine.
== Shrine Grounds == <gallery widths="200px" heights="150px" perrow="3"> File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall </gallery>
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes.

== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588824}}, Volume 5,''<ref>''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.''</ref> which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワクムスビ}}.<ref>The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>
According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942.
=== Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine.
== Shrine Grounds == <gallery widths="200px" heights="150px" perrow="3"> File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall </gallery>
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes.
== Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963.
{{Omonoimi Faith}}

Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[:en:Tobishima, Yamagata|Tobishima (Yamagata)]], an isolated island in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was District Shrine.<ref>In January
1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine.</ref> Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City.

It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588824}}, Volume 5,''<ref>''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.''</ref> which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワクムスビ}}.<ref>The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>
According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942.
=== Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine.
== Shrine Grounds == <gallery widths="200px" heights="150px" perrow="3"> File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall </gallery>
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes.
== Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963.
{{Omonoimi Faith}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Tobishima]][[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11462864]] Wikidata name: Omonoimi-jinja, Tobishima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG |location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|39.19591666666667|N|139.55369444444443|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|WD=Q1073925}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}|prefecture= {{ill|Sakata|WD=Q504690}} |architecture_style= {{ill|rake-overhanging gabled roof|WD=Q13308575}} }}
Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[:en:Tobishima, Yamagata|Tobishima (Yamagata)]], an isolated island in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was District Shrine.<ref>In January
1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine.</ref> Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City.
== Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588824}}, Volume 5,''<ref>''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.''</ref> which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワクムスビ}}.<ref>The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]],'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>
According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942.
=== Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine.
== Shrine Grounds == <gallery widths="200px" heights="150px" perrow="3"> File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall </gallery>
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Port of Sakata|Sakata Port]], take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes.
== Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963.

Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] [[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] [[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

'''Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Dewa Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|lt=Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Tobishima|WD=Q11462864}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Womonoimino Shrine|lt=Womonoimino Shrine|WD=Q135040052}}; {{ill|Womonoimino Shrine|lt=Womonoimino Shrine|WD=Q135040052}} || {{coord|39.195741|139.553853|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182139 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11462864}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:2]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:4]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] [[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Tobishima).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, Tobishima, Nakamura Kō 178 |coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-06|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内小社論社|qq=draft}}, Former District Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= April 20 |Rituals = Chōkai-san Hi-awase Ritual (July 14) |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|marksize=2|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center}}}} Komonoi Shrine (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Tobishima (Yamagata)|ja|飛島 (山形県)|qq=draft}}, an isolated island in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] [[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was District Shrine.In January 1946, it was recognized as equivalent to a Prefectural Shrine Rank (''Shrine Registry''). In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' it is noted that the shrine may have been listed as a Village Shrine or Prefectural Shrine during its time as Ōmiya Shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists within Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|ja|級長戸辺命|qq=draft}} == History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Shrine tradition claims it was enshrined from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}. It is considered a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} listed in the ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|ja|神名帳考証|qq=draft}}, Volume 5,''''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' is included in ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1.'' which states that the Komonoi Shrine now located on Tobishima enshrines a wind deity, identified in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}'' as {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|qq=draft}}.The wind deity mentioned is the same as the deity enshrined at Tatsuta Taisha. In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] In the ''Kojiki,'' the wind deity is named Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}},'' this deity is written as Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto. This name is an alternate for Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, born from the breath of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊奘諾尊|qq=draft}} as he dispelled morning mist (''Shin Birth Section, Variant 6''). Wakumusubi is a deity of grains and sericulture and the parent deity of {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比売命|qq=draft}}. According to the ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' in 1738, the shrine's name was changed to Ōmiya Shrine, prompting many other shrines to adopt the name Komonoi Shrine without substantiation. To address this, Shirazaki Yoshimi, director of the Sakata City Kōkyū Bunko Foundation, conducted a personal investigation and concluded that Ōmiya Shrine on Tobishima was the true Komonoi Shrine. Through his appeals, the shrine's name was officially restored to Komonoi Shrine on July 10, 1942. === Timeline === * Said to have been enshrined from Tatsuta Taisha. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Ninth Court Rank (applies to the Shikinai Komonoi Shrine). * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank (same as above). * 1738 - Renamed Ōmiya Shrine. * July 10, 1942 - name restored to Komonoi Shrine. == Shrine Grounds == File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Torii File:Precincts of Omonoimi-jinja (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Precincts File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Tobishima).JPG|Komonoi Shrine Main Hall == Transportation == * From {{ill|Sakata Port|ja|酒田港|qq=draft}}, take the regular ferry ''Tobishima'' to Katsuura Port (1 trip per day, approximately 75 minutes travel time), then walk about 20 minutes. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki (ed.), ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. * Shrine Headquarters Research Division (ed.), ''Shrine Registry,'' Shrine Headquarters, 1963. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former District Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] [[ja:小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] [[sn:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[en:Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
